originalBehavior: The current live simulating route is a lightweight launcher that points readers to the new Emoji Simulator and the old original version.
localStatus: The local launcher keeps that lightweight two-link behavior while routing to the local top-level sim/ route and the nested legacy original/ route.
notes: The launcher intentionally stays simpler than the article or editor surfaces.
Public provenance for the launcher is limited to the shared footer reference link.
evidence: Live launcher behavior was verified against https://ncase.me/simulating/ on 2026-03-10.
Local smoke coverage checks that the launcher links route to the local sim/ and simulating/original/ paths.

originalBehavior: The old original version is a long-form explorable article with splash art, text sections, and several embedded playable simulations.
localStatus: The local legacy article keeps the original narrative flow, splash section, and long-form markup while moving metadata and provenance to the local shell plus shared footer.
notes: The local copy keeps the article content intact rather than rebuilding it in a different framework.
The visible byline, share block, creator promo, and supporter-heavy ending were removed from the public surface.
evidence: The copied article still mounts the same splash and example structure from the upstream release.
Local smoke coverage verifies that the article route loads and that the zoo example switcher still updates the active embedded model.

originalBehavior: The legacy article embeds multiple iframes from the sibling model editor and updates example links as readers switch between zoo presets.
localStatus: All embedded article iframes now point to the nested local ../model/ surface, and the zoo example link generation remains upstream-driven through the original helper script.
notes: The local port kept the original scripts/index.js behavior instead of reimplementing the zoo picker or embed helper.
Static article markup was patched so the generated open-in-new-tab and embed URLs stay under the local subpath.
evidence: The recheck plan explicitly called out simulating/original/ and simulating/model/ as nested parity targets.
Local smoke coverage checks that the zoo iframe source changes and that the generated example link points to the local nested model route.

originalBehavior: The legacy model editor lets readers play, pause, reset, draw, edit rule text, save remote links, and export JSON.
localStatus: The local nested model editor preserves the original control bar, editor sidebar, remote-save hook, and JSON export behavior while removing public GitHub promo copy.
notes: The Firebase-backed remote save code remains in place because it is part of the original editor runtime.
The local cleanup only neutralized the export helper copy and added footer-aware shell metadata.
evidence: The current plan treats the nested model editor as parity-critical for the Simulating bundle.
Local smoke coverage exercises play or pause, brush changes, a stubbed save-success flow, and JSON export from the nested editor.

originalBehavior: The original article ended with educational reading links plus creator, share, and supporter callouts.
localStatus: The public tail now keeps only educational references plus local internal links to the nested model editor and the current Emoji Simulator.
notes: Subject-matter references were preserved because they are part of the article content.
Creator, repo, contact, and supporter blocks were removed to enforce the footer-only provenance rule.
evidence: The current public-surface policy allows educational references but removes original-site promo or supporter surfaces.
Audit coverage now includes the shipped simulating route tree and its nested HTML or JS files.
